value: true });\nconst cli_1 = require(\"@tsoa/cli\");\nif (require.main === module)\n    void (0, cli_1.runCLI)();\n//# sourceMappingURL=cli.js.map\n```\n\n**Summary of the code:**\n- It is a standard Node.js CLI bootstrap script.\n- It imports the `runCLI` function from the `@tsoa/cli` package.\n- If executed directly (`require.main === module`), it calls `runCLI()`.\n- It contains a source map comment, indicating it might be transpiled or compiled code.\n\n---\n\n### Review of the reports:\n\n**General observations:**\n- All reports agree that the code is straightforward, minimal, and standard for a CLI entry point.\n- No anomalies, obfuscation, or suspicious patterns are noted.\n- The code does not process external data directly, nor does it perform network operations.\n- The `runCLI()` function is called only when the script is run directly, which is typical behavior.\n\n**Security implications:**\n- Since `runCLI()` is invoked from a trusted package (`@tsoa/cli`), and the code itself is a simple bootstrap, there is no inherent security risk.\n- No hardcoded secrets, no data leaks, and no suspicious code are present.\n\n**Malware and obfuscation
